items.description: Punishment for murder; whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder.
Culpable homicide not amounting to murder; punishment includes life imprisonment or imprisonment up to 10 years and fine.

Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person.
Designates specific severe injuries (e.g., emasculation, permanent privation of sight/hearing, fracture) and provides punishment for voluntarily causing them.
Voluntarily causing grievous hurt by use of acid, etc.; punishable with imprisonment of not less than 10 years which may extend to life.
Voluntarily causing hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as weapon of offence, is likely to cause death.
Kidnapping from India or from lawful guardianship; punishable with imprisonment up to seven years and fine.
Whoever by force compels, or by any deceitful means induces, any person to go from any place.
Taking or enticing any minor (under 16 for males, 18 for females) out of the keeping of the lawful guardian without consent.
Punishment for theft; whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment up to three years, or with fine, or with both.
Theft is robbery if the offender causes or attempts to cause death, hurt, or wrongful restraint.
When five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit a robbery.
Entering into or upon property in the possession of another with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate/insult.
Effecting entrance into a house or any part of it in any of the six ways described in the section for the purpose of committing an offence.
Mischief by fire or explosive substance with intent to cause damage to property or destruction of a house.
Causing the destruction of property or any such change in property as destroys or diminishes its value or utility.
Whenever force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly, or by any member thereof, in prosecution of the common object.
Historically prosecuted under Murder/Rioting sections; under new laws (BNS), it is specifically addressed as murder committed by a group on grounds of race, caste, etc.
Physical contact involving unwelcome sexual overtures, demanding sexual favors, showing pornography against a person's will, or making sexually colored remarks.
Following a woman and contacting or attempting to contact her repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest; also includes monitoring her internet or electronic communication.
Using words, gestures, or sounds intended to insult the modesty of a woman or intruding upon her privacy.
Harassment of a woman by her husband or his relatives, particularly to coerce her to meet unlawful demands for property or dowry.
Threatening a person with injury to their person, reputation, or property with intent to cause alarm or force them to do/omit any act.
Performing obscene acts in public or singing/uttering obscene songs or words in or near a public place to the annoyance of others.
Watching or capturing the image of a woman engaging in a private act where she would usually expect privacy.
